messages.content:
edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel:
And the guide starts talking again with enthusiasm
about how to rebuild Persia from ruins to its well-deserved
reputation from 150 years ago when we are so fascinated by
looking into the mirror of history that we lose the tour guide
and the crowd we followed until suddenly, Gabriel appears
to both of us and say, “Sometimes, history is incomplete or
distorted, and finding the truth beyond it, is tedious and
challenging. This country’s history dates back to when the
Aryans, 15,000 years before what is called Persia today,
migrated to Europe.
“It was at the time that the vast sea (and now a small
part of it remains as like a desert in the east) was evacuated
of by a great earthquake. Many people were forced to
migrate north and west in search of new resources.

And so, as the guide's enthusiasm reaches its peak, he passionately embarks on a discourse about the arduous task of resurrecting Persia from the ashes of ruins to regain its long-lost prestige from a bygone era; a time when history held a profound allure for us, causing us to unintentionally drift away from the tour group, losing sight of our guide amidst the crowd. 

Suddenly, a figure materializes before us, none other than Gabriel, who meets our surprised gaze and utters words of wisdom, "Indeed, history can be an incomplete and distorted tapestry, obfuscating the truth that lies beyond its veiled facade. Discovering the authentic narrative concealed within proves to be an endeavor tedious and arduous in nature. For this nation's storied chronicles trace back to a time when the Aryans, a remarkable 15,000 years prior to the present-day Persia, ventured westward into Europe."

A pivotal moment arrived when a catastrophic earthquake shook the earth's bowels, resulting in the evacuation of the expansive sea that now sits as but a vestige, resembling a desolate desert in its eastern region. This cataclysmic event thrust multitudes of people into compelled migration, their only salvation found by journeying northward and westward, in search of new resources to sustain their existence.

edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel:
“Much farther back then, the generation that lived on
earth was at the height of their civilization and technology.
However, they became so greedy that they consumed all
their resources and created pollution so that global warming
gradually changed the climate to such an extent that the
glaciers melted and suddenly the great flood swept through
the earth.”

Centuries earlier, a generation thrived upon this once bountiful Earth, reaching the apex of civilization and technological prowess. Alas, their insatiable greed plagued their every decision. Their voracious consumption of resources, driven by an insidious hunger for more, led them down a treacherous path. Annoyed by their ceaseless transgressions, the planet succumbed to festering pollution, casting a somber shadow upon their future.

Inescapably, the climate metamorphosed, manipulated by the stealthy fingers of global warming. As the temperature soared to unrestrained heights, the serene glaciers gradually succumbed, cascading into oblivion. Then, like an enraged titan, the mighty floodwaters surged forth, engulfing the once-glorious domains of the earth, relentless and unforgiving in their wrath.

edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel: 
“I don’t understand it well, now, man is civilized, but at
that time, the man had to be primitive?” I speak.
Gabriel, like a veteran professor who is teaching to a
bunch of kids, smiles and kindly explains, “You’re the
fourth generation of humans to ever live on Earth up to now.
Civilization on Earth has gone to dusk a few times and come
back again from The Stone Age to a civilization that was
even more advanced than of yours today.
“Some immigrated from Earth to other planets, and
some other annihilated once by a great flood, other times by
huge earthquakes or, being on the direct hit of the X-ray of
a supernova that is alongside of the Earth.” Gabriel
continues.

Puzzled by the perplexities of the past, I voice my confusion aloud, seeking clarity. "I struggle to comprehend this phenomenon. In our present era, mankind is considered civilized, but in those bygone days, were they steeped in primitivism?" 

Like a sagacious professor imparting wisdom to a group of impressionable children, Gabriel's countenance radiates with benevolence as he graciously elucidates the enigma. "You, dear traveler, are but the fourth generation of humankind to tread upon the hallowed grounds of Earth. Civilization, in its ebb and flow, has witnessed dusk several times before, only to reemerge with renewed vigor. From the primordial era of The Stone Age, humanity evolved into a civilization surpassing even the apex of advancement achieved in your present time."

He continues, his voice bearing the weight of wisdom borne from countless ages. "Countless souls sought refuge upon alien planets, bidding farewell to their ancestral home. Yet, other civilizations were vanquished, as the colossal forces of nature unleashed torrents of deluge or cataclysmic earthquakes. And let us not forget the treacherous brush with annihilation, the Earth subjected to the intense onslaught of a supernova's X-ray, ravaging all within its unforgiving reach."

Gabriel's discourse offers a captivating glimpse into the tumultuous cycles of civilizations that have graced this ancient sphere.

edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel: I ask again, “Why the survivors still have to rediscover
the fire?”
“Every catastrophic phenomenon changes the climate
drastically,” says Gabriel. “After the massive floods,
massive earthquakes, or terrible volcanoes, the diameter of
the clouds of smoke increased to 10 km thick. The survivors
were in the dark.”
“They lived in dark, days and nights, and because of the
terrible shock of the massive catastrophe, their memory was
lost partly or erased, which is why they had to rediscover
everything from the beginning. And one of the first events
that caught their attention was fire, light—the fire that begot
by lightning or a volcano from time to time.
“But the worst was the x-ray from a supernova close to
Earth that emptied the planet from every living entity, and
inevitably the evolution had to start from the beginning,
unicellular organisms.”

Once again, the lingering question tumbles from my lips, persistence etched upon my face. "Why is it that even the survivors must embark anew on the quest to rediscover the elemental force of fire?"

Gabriel, his countenance a captivating tableau of knowledge, responds with measured words. "Every cataclysmic event births a monumental shift in the climate's tapestry," he explains. "When the colossal floods, devastating earthquakes, or ravenous volcanoes wreak havoc, an ominous, impenetrable shroud of smoke engulfs the land, its dark tendrils extending up to ten kilometers in thickness. The survivors, veiled in perpetual darkness, are robbed of illumination."

He pauses for a breath, his eyes glistening with the weight of history. "Days blend into nights seamlessly, indistinguishable within the consuming darkness. The sheer magnitude of the cataclysmic upheaval decimates their memories, erasing fragments of their collective past. It is in this fragmented void that they ardently embark upon the arduous task of rediscovery, commencing their journey from the very beginning. And among the first miracles to seize their attention is the mesmerizing dance of flames - the beacon of fire, the embodiment of light. It is born anew sporadically, whether ignited through the fury of lightning or birthed by the fierce belly of a volatile volcano."

His voice fills the air, carrying an undercurrent of awe. "Yet, amidst this tapestry of tribulations, one stands unmatched in its cataclysmic magnitude. The x-ray unleashed by a neighboring supernova, with brutal efficiency, emptied the planet of every living creature. Inevitably, the cycle began anew, tracing lineage from the humble origins of unicellular organisms."

Gabriel's words paint a breathtaking portrait of a world reborn, each calamity birthing a fresh genesis, weaving a tapestry where forgotten memories intertwine with the resilient spirit of humanity's inexorable evolution.

edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel: Gabriel says, “Discoveries like nuclear structure and
much of the technology that you are familiar with today are
new findings of the present generation, whereas food, water,
fire, and shelter were the essential needs of primitive man.
These late discoveries were not felt or seen visually or
mentally since they were not their primary needs.
Therefore, they couldn’t sense the necessity of modern
technology or pay much attention to them until 500 years
ago that man started a new scientific era after the
renaissance.”

Gabriel's expression melds reverence and wisdom as he imparts his insight. "Marvels such as the intricate nuclear structure and the very foundations of the technology that now inhabits your realm, are but recent discoveries gifted to the present generation. However, it is important to understand that the fundamental requisites of existence for primitive man were encompassed by food, water, fire, and shelter. These primal needs enveloped their entire existence, forging a different perspective wherein the value and essence of modern technology may not have resonated as intensely."

He pauses, allowing his words to linger in the air before continuing. "These revolutionary discoveries, born of a later age, may not have been visible to their senses or grasped mentally since they did not rank as primary necessities of their lives. It was not until the dawn of the Renaissance, roughly five centuries ago, that mankind embarked upon a new era of scientific exploration, birthing a profound shift in their understanding and appreciation for the complexities of the world."

Gabriel's words evoke a profound realization, unraveling the inherent human propensity to prioritize survival and sustenance over the uncharted realms of technological advancement. It is within this context that the significance and impact of the wonders of modern science gradually unveiled themselves to human consciousness throughout history.

edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel: 
We are walking toward the car that is waiting to take us
to lunch and talking with Gabriel at the same time. When
we get into the vehicle, the driver and the host who are from
the US Consulate notice me talking to Carol, but she is
watching the views outside and not responding to me when,
in fact, I am engaged asking questions and talking to
Gabriel.
The host takes us to one of the most famous restaurants
in Shiraz, where the US Consul has invited us for lunch.

As we stride purposefully towards the awaiting vehicle, our conversation with Gabriel persists, unfazed by the transition. Taking our places inside the car, the attentive gaze of both the driver and the host from the esteemed US Consulate falls upon me, noticing my animated interaction with Carol. Yet, she remains transfixed by the scenic vistas that unfold beyond the windowpane, her attention seemingly oblivious to my inquiries and conversation with Gabriel.

Undeterred by her contemplative silence, I continue delving into a realm of questions and discussions with Gabriel. Our dialogue, ripe with intrigue, continues to unfold as the car weaves through the vibrant streets of Shiraz. Soon, we arrive at our destination, a renowned restaurant embraced by the invitation of the US Consul. Its prestigious reputation precedes it, promising a memorable dining experience within its walls.

edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel: 
the driver and the host who are from
the US Consulate notice me talking to Carol, but she is
watching the views outside and not responding to me when,
in fact, I am engaged asking questions and talking to
Gabriel.

As we approach the waiting vehicle, captivated in conversation with Gabriel, my attention becomes caught on the observant gaze of the driver and the host, both esteemed representatives of the US Consulate. Their piercing eyes inadvertently lock onto me, their curiosity piqued by the animated exchange unfolding between Carol and me. However, I am taken aback as I realize that her gaze lies fixated on the sprawling views outside, seemingly oblivious to my inquiries and the enthralling dialogue shared with Gabriel.

Undeterred by her ephemeral detachment, I persevere in my quest for knowledge, immersing myself in a captivating exchange with Gabriel. Our words weave a tapestry of curiosity and insight, as if unraveling an enigmatic story amidst the vibrant streets that unfurl beyond the car's windowpane. While Carol remains an emancipated bystander to our discourse, my engagement intensifies, fueling a dynamic exchange and an eagerness to unearth deeper truths.

In the realm of the fantastical, where the seeking of knowledge intertwines with the enigma of human connections, the mystifying interaction between Carol's distant contemplation and my impassioned conversation with Gabriel casts a spell, their significance yet to be fully unveiled.

revise this sentence: The host takes us to one of the most famous restaurants
in Shiraz, where the US Consul has invited us for lunch.

Guided by the hospitable host from the US Consulate, we embark on a culinary adventure to one of Shiraz's renowned establishments, graciously extended by the generous invitation of the esteemed US Consul.

edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel: I ask Gabriel again, “Would you tell me why there are
contradictions between Charles Darwin’s theory of
evolution and the story of Eve and Adam in many
scriptures?
Gabriel answers, “Every colossal catastrophe might
take mankind back to the beginning of his evolution. Life
starts first from a unicellular organism, and through
millions and millions of years of evolution, Neanderthals
and later Homo-Sapiens, through a mutation process,
become human.
“Darwin’s discovery is a significant advancement in
science; Natural Selection is the primary rule of evolution
and survival. The necessities that the environment forces
upon living creatures to change. But what he couldn’t
explain precisely was the mutation of two Homo-Sapiens to
two humans, ‘Adam’ and ‘Eve.’ And how and by what plan
the mutation happened.”

Seeking answers to the perplexing conundrum that has long intrigued me, I pose my query once more to Gabriel. "Could you shed light upon the intriguing contradictions that exist between Charles Darwin's theory of evolution and the ancient tale of Eve and Adam found in various scriptures?"

With an aura of profound wisdom, Gabriel begins to unravel the intricacies of this enigmatic topic. "With every cataclysmic upheaval that befalls mankind, there exists the potential for our evolutionary journey to be reset back to its humble beginnings. Life emerges, initially, from the humble realms of unicellular organisms. Through countless eons of evolution, including the emergence of Neanderthals and the subsequent ascent of Homo sapiens through the transformative forces of mutation, humanity flourishes."

Gabriel's voice resonates with a deep reverence for scientific progress as he continues, "Darwin's remarkable discoveries signify a monumental leap forward for scientific understanding. Natural Selection, the cornerstone of evolution and survival, dictates the transformative changes thrust upon living beings by their environment. However, it is in the precise genesis of two Homo sapiens mutating into the fabled figures of 'Adam' and 'Eve' that Darwin's theory falters. The intricate workings and intentions behind this unparalleled mutation remain elusive, leaving unanswered questions about the precise plan that orchestrated this momentous transformation."

With each word, Gabriel's profound insight weaves a mesmerizing tapestry, revealing the intricate dance between scientific revelations and the timeless mysteries enshrined within ancient narratives. The compelling union of truth and myth beckons us closer, offering glimpses of a larger, mysterious design yet to be fully comprehended.

edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel: 
I ask quickly, “What is the cause of mutation besides
the environment?”
“An intelligent human can affect the mutations on other
animals. The same thing happens to other creatures. Just
look at how the discoveries and innovations of noble
scientists have changed your life. As you can see, a female
gorilla learns sign language (Hanabiko ‘Koko’) from a
noble lady (Francine Patterson), or many pets learn many
words as a result of living with humans.” And this is the
story of Adam and Eve.”

Eager to delve into the depths of knowledge, my words spring forth in rapid succession. "Beyond the influence of the environment, can there be other factors that give rise to mutation?"

Gabriel responds with a contemplative mien, his words resonating with profound insight. "Indeed, the power of an intelligent human extends beyond the realm of their own species. The noble innovations and discoveries crafted by remarkable scientists have the potential to manifest significant mutations in other creatures. Observing the impact of these advancements on your own life serves as a testament to the profound transformations that can occur. Just consider the remarkable tale of a female gorilla named Hanabiko 'Koko', who acquired the ability to communicate using sign language under the tutelage of the noble and compassionate Francine Patterson. Similarly, many cherished pets have acquired an extensive vocabulary by virtue of their intimate coexistence with humans."

Gabriel's words paint a captivating image, illustrating the immense potential for learning, growth, and adaptation that emerges from the profound connection between intelligent beings. It is within this context that the story of Adam and Eve finds its place; a timeless narrative rooted in the extraordinary potential residing in the relationship between beings, transcending the boundaries of species and intertwining the tapestry of existence.

edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel: I ask again, “Who was the first, the intelligent human
who taught others words and afterward writing?”
Gabriel says, “There is always one smartest or visionary
person who lives in every period, even if the population is
made up of just one person, and you shouldn’t ask me where
or who is this human, This is what you have to find it
yourself.”

Eager for insight into the origins of knowledge and language, I pose my inquiry once more to Gabriel. "Who was the progenitor, the very first intelligent human who bestowed the gift of words and, subsequently, writing upon others?"

Without a hint of hesitation, Gabriel responds, his voice filled with a profound wisdom. "In every era, there exists an individual who shines as the wisest or forges a visionary path ahead, even if the collective population consists of only one person. However, my dear seeker of truth, it is not for me to reveal the precise whereabouts or identity of this exceptional being. This is a quest that lies within the depths of your heart, waiting for your personal exploration and discovery."

Gabriel's words resonate, carrying with them the gentle admonition of
